q: All chemical reactions go to completion.
Some reactions do not go to completion.
A reversible reaction is a one-way reaction.
Reversible reaction can proceed in the forward direction only.
A chemical equilibrium is said to be a dynamic nature.
The reaction proceeding to the right is called the reverse reaction.
Equilibrium reactions are reversible.
At equilibrium, all reactants have been converted to products.
When a reaction is at equilibrium, it has no reactants.
When the rates of the forward and reverse reactions balance, the reversible reaction is in equilibrium.
The reaction is completely stop at the equilibrium.
Neither the forward nor the backward reaction is proceeding at equilibrium.
A closed system is one in which neither the reactants or the products can escape from the container.
In a closed system, a reversible reaction reaches equilibrium with both reactants and products present.
In an equilibrium process, the concentrations of products and reactants are equal.
In a dynamic equilibrium, the concentrations of reactants and products remains constant.
A reaction is at equilibrium when no reactants remain.
A system that has achieved chemical equilibrium will have constant concentrations of reactants and products.
No net change in concentration of reactants and products are observed at equilibrium.
At equilibrium, the forward and reverse reactions are still proceeding at an equal rate.
The equilibrium in which reactants are products are in single phase is called homogeneous chemical equilibrium.
The equilibrium in which reactants and products are in single phase is called heterogeneous chemical equilibrium.
N2(g) + 3H2(g) = 2NH3(g); It is a heterogeneous equilibrium reaction.
CO2(g) + C(s) = 2CO(g) is an example of homogeneous equilibrium.
A saturated solution is in heterogeneous equilibrium.
The equilibrium between water and its vapour in an open vessel can be achieved.
Le Chatelier Principle is applicable to dynamic equilibrium.
Once equilibrium is established in a system, further change is apparent as long as the external factors remain unchanged.
Addition of catalyst changes the position of equilibrium.
Le Chatelier's principle describes shifts in equilibrium concentrations.
A catalyst does not affect the equilibrium concentrations and there is no change in equilibrium.
An increase in temperature would favour the exothermic reaction.
A change in the concentration of one of the substances in an equilibrium system affects the equilibrium positions.
Changing the temperature cannot affect the equilibrium position.
The formation of products is favoured by decreasing temperature in an endothermic reaction.
The effect of changing the pressure on the reaction just involving liquids is negligible.
As pressure increases, the equilibrium moves toward the side of the reaction with more moles of gas.
At constant temperature, the pressure is directly proportional to the concentration of the gas.
The amount of product present is no longer affected by changing in temperature or pressure at equilibrium.
The amount of product present is only affected by a change in pressure at equilibrium.
The rate of the forward reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of the products.
Equilibrium constant depends on the temperature.
The value of Keq is constant at different temperature.
The value of equilibrium constant for a chemical reaction at a particular temperature is constant.
The equilibrium constant changes with changes in the temperature.
If Keq << 1, then at equilibrium there are more products than reactants.
If the equilibrium constant, Keq, is very large, more products are will be formed.
At equilibrium, Keq is equal to 1.
The units of Kp and Kc are equal.
Kp and Kc can be applied for the gaseous reactions.
